heading: Usage
body:
To retrieve paginated results, perform the following steps:

- [create a MongoDB Search index](ref-create-index) on the fields you want to query.

- Run a [$search](pipe.$search) query that returns a point of reference. To
  learn more, see [Retrieve Point of Reference](paginate-results-search-sequence-token).

- Use the point of reference in your subsequent [$search](pipe.$search)
  query to retrieve the next or previous set of documents in the
  results.

  - To learn more about retrieving results to build a "Next
    Page" function, see [retrieve search results after a reference point](paginate-results-search-after).

  - To learn more about retrieving results to build a "Previous Page"
    function, see [Search Before a Specific Point of Reference](paginate-results-search-before).

  - To jump to a page in your results, combine [$skip](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/skip) and
    [$limit](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/limit) with [$search](pipe.$search) `searchAfter` or
    `searchBefore` options. For example, to jump to results from Page
    3 to Page 5, with 10 results per page, do the following:

    - Retrieve results using `searchAfter` with the point of
      reference for the last result on Page 3 (Result 30).

    - Use [$skip](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/skip) to skip the 10 results on Page 4 (Results
      31-40) and [$limit](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/limit) to limit the results to 10
      documents.

    - Return results for Page 5 (Results 41-50).

    Here, using [$skip](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/skip) with the `searchAfter` option optimizes
    the query to skip only 1 page of results (10 documents). By comparison,
    if you use `$skip` without the [$search](pipe.$search) `searchAfter`
    option, the query skips 4 pages of results (40 documents). To learn
    more, see [Jump from Page 2 to Page 5 Using searchAfter and $skip](sequential-pagination-tutorial-skip-query).
